rows.notes: Very clear printed year at top of page, centered and unambiguous.
Clear heading text with underline; handwriting is neat and fully legible.

rows.description: Dr. Proprietors & Tenants one years Rent Charges to Wm? 1834
rows.notes: Amount columns aligned under the right ruling lines and read as 220 0 0 clearly. Description wording is partly obscured/ornate script: 'Proprietors & Tenants' and 'one years' are legible; following name 'Wm?' and exact last letters uncertain.

rows.description: Interest on Monthly Deposit 4? 17? 5? 3 pr cent
rows.notes: Amount reads as 5 pounds, 18 shillings, 8 pence; alignment with column lines is moderate but pence digit slightly smudged (could be 7 or 8), chosen 8 based on position under pence column. Description is partly faded; I transcribed approximate words 'Interest on Monthly Deposit' and left the numeric/annotation fragment uncertain.

rows.description: Mr. Gilmore Insurance Dividends to March 1837
rows.notes: Pounds-shillings-pence columns are well aligned and read clearly as 428 6 8. Name and 'Insurance'/'Dividends' wording readable but ornate; year 'March 1837' visible. Minor uncertainty in exact spelling of the surname.

Numeric columns show 16 8 7 but the pence digit slightly faint; chosen 7 based on alignment with pence column. 'Whitlock' reading uncertain — script could be 'Whitelock' or 'Whitelost'; prefixed by 'Do.' (ditto) is clear.

rows.description: Receipt full of improved rents from Ridford
rows.notes: Amount columns read clearly as 29 6 8. Description 'improved rents' and 'from' are legible; the place-name looks like 'Ridford' but could be slightly different (handwriting flourish on middle letters).

rows.description: Do. Do. from Medenham to Mars 1836
rows.notes: Amounts 15 13 10 are well aligned and distinct. The line begins with 'Do.' (ditto) twice then place-name that reads 'Medenham' or similar and the year '1836' (written as 'Mars' for March) — wording mostly legible though the place spelling uncertain.

rows.description: Of Mr Beaton(?) Guttinhalde, Mr ? rent for the season of the State
rows.notes: Amount 5 3 6 reads under the correct columns but description is moderately unclear: several proper names (Mr Beaton?, 'Guttinhalde'?) are difficult to decipher; context suggests a rent item. Chose the punctuation/spacing to reflect uncertain reading.

rows.description: Of Mr A? on the Library acct as part of the Fund's Bill
rows.notes: Amounts 84 5 7 align clearly with ruling lines. The description references a library account/fund bill but the name (Mr A...) is faint and the exact phrasing is slightly ambiguous; chosen reading based on nearby words and context.

rows.description: Reimbursement to Domes from St. Newington Rents 1/2 year to 1 Day 1837
rows.notes: Amount 45 0 0 is crisp and unambiguous. Description 'Reimbursement to Domes from St. Newington' and the rental period '1/2 year to Day 1837' are mostly legible; minor uncertainty only in exact punctuation and abbreviation 'Domes'.

rows.description: Of Mr Dayman, Surveyor, Incrementums Domes to Mid? 1838
rows.notes: Large amount 840 6 10 is clearly aligned and legible. The description contains names/titles ('Mr Dayman', possibly 'Surveyor') and words like 'Incrementums' which are written in a flowing script and partially uncertain; year '1838' indicated. Chosen spelling reflects best reading.

Amount 26 0 10 is clear in columns. Description begins with 'Of Do.' (ditto) and mentions 'Office of Billets' — the word 'Billets' is slightly stylized but readable. Minor uncertainty about exact capitalization/spacing.

rows.description: Mr. Dawson Dean's Fees 1/2 to Mich? 1838
rows.notes: Amount 38 17 4 is well aligned and distinct. The name 'Mr. Dawson' and 'Dean's Fees' are legible; the trailing date/abbreviation 'Mich? 1838' (March 1838) is slightly abbreviated but clear enough to transcribe as shown.
Final total at bottom right is clearly written and underlined. Pounds, shillings and pence columns line up and read as 1755 4 6. Very little ambiguity in the numerals or layout.
